opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I haven't slept in two days, my heart is pounding out of my chest, and I just know the tests are going to say I have cancer.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: I'm waiting on blood test results and I'm terrified they'll show something awful. I've been panicking all night, my heart is racing and I can't calm down.

symptom_narrative.onset: over the last two days, since I've been waiting for my results to come back
symptom_narrative.character: heart pounding and racing, can't sleep at all, feeling sick in the stomach, shaky and on edge
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst anxiety I've ever felt, I'm convinced something terrible is coming
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: pretty much constant, much worse whenever I start thinking about the results
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing really; my spouse keeps trying to reassure me but it doesn't help for long
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: thinking about the results, and looking up my symptoms online
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: pounding heart, feeling sick, not able to sleep, trembling, pacing around
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can't sleep, can't sit still, can't really function

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: I've been really tired and lost a bit of weight lately, which is why my doctor ordered the blood tests this week.

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives with a supportive spouse who is with me right now

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: wants urgent reassurance that the results are not going to be cancer

communication_style.typical_message_style: rapid, fearful, catastrophic interpretation of bodily sensations

objective_data_offered.value: my spouse says I'm pacing, trembling and sweaty and can't settle
my heart feels like it's pounding out of my chest
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient-reported sensation of palpitations, not a measured heart rate
